## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On October 2, 1968, an incredibly massive, absolutely horrifying, and deeply tragic political massacre aggressively occurred in Mexico City. Just ten days before the highly anticipated Summer Olympics, heavily armed Mexican military and police forces fiercely opened fire on a massive, peaceful assembly of student protesters in the Plaza de las Tres Culturas (Tlatelolco). The incredibly brutal, massive state-sponsored slaughter resulted in the horrific deaths of hundreds of unarmed students, deeply traumatizing the nation and forever staining the absolute legacy of the PRI government.

On October 2, 1925, John Logie Baird, an incredibly brilliant, pioneering Scottish inventor, successfully and aggressively achieved an absolute, monumental technological breakthrough in his London laboratory. He aggressively transmitted the absolute, very first recognizable television image with massive grayscale halftones (the head of a ventriloquist's dummy). This incredible, massive engineering triumph fundamentally laid the absolute, architectural bedrock for the massive global television industry, aggressively launching the most massive, highly influential mass communication medium of the 20th century.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On October 2, 1869, Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi, the absolute, incredibly massive, and universally revered "Father of the Nation," was officially born in Porbandar, Gujarat. His incredibly massive, historically unprecedented philosophy of *Satyagraha* (non-violent resistance) aggressively and fundamentally shattered the absolute foundation of the British Empire. By heavily mobilizing the massive, deeply impoverished Indian masses, his absolute, uncompromising spiritual and political leadership successfully secured Indian independence, establishing him as one of the most massive, iconic figures in modern human history.

On October 2, 1904, Lal Bahadur Shastri, the incredibly massive, deeply principled, and highly respected 2nd Prime Minister of India, was officially born in Mughalsarai. Assuming absolute leadership following the massive death of Jawaharlal Nehru, his incredibly brief but heavily impactful tenure was absolutely defined by his massive, aggressive leadership during the highly volatile 1965 Indo-Pakistani War. His incredibly massive, universally iconic rallying cry, "Jai Jawan Jai Kisan," heavily unified the absolute, massive agrarian and military backbone of the republic.
